skill.name: redesign-existing-projects
skill.description: Upgrades existing websites and apps to premium quality. Audits current design, identifies generic AI patterns, and applies high-end design standards without breaking functionality. Works with any CSS framework or vanilla CSS.
skill.summary: Transform generic AI-generated designs into premium, polished interfaces by auditing and upgrading existing projects.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: All 77 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner treated Markdown documentation as executable code. The 49 external_commands detections are Markdown backtick syntax for inline code references (CSS properties, font names like Geist, Outfit). The 27 blocker detections for weak cryptographic algorithms and system reconnaissance have no basis in the file content, which contains only design guidelines and audit checklists. The single network detection is a documentation reference to picsum.photos, a placeholder image service. No executable code, scripts, or network calls exist in this skill. It is a pure Markdown document providing design improvement guidance.

content.user_title: Upgrade existing projects with premium design standards
content.value_statement: AI-generated websites often share the same generic patterns, making them look indistinguishable. This skill audits your existing project and applies targeted design upgrades to create a polished, premium look without rewriting your codebase.
actual_capabilities: Audit existing codebases for common design problems like generic fonts, oversaturated colors, and symmetrical layouts
Identify and replace cliched AI design patterns such as purple gradients, three-card carousels, and pill-shaped badges
Apply targeted CSS upgrades including font swaps, color palette refinement, and hover state additions
Improve interactivity with proper loading, empty, and error states for forms and dashboards
Upgrade typography with better font pairings, letter-spacing adjustments, and text wrapping fixes
Work with any existing CSS framework including Tailwind, styled-components, or vanilla CSS
limitations: Does not rewrite frameworks or migrate styling libraries, only improves what already exists
Requires an existing codebase with HTML and CSS to audit and upgrade
Design improvements are applied as code changes, not visual mockups or Figma files
Cannot guarantee pixel-perfect results across all browsers without manual testing

use_cases.title: Polish a portfolio or marketing site
use_cases.description: Take a functional but generic website and upgrade it with premium typography, custom color palettes, and sophisticated layout patterns that stand out from template designs.

use_cases.title: Upgrade an AI-generated application
use_cases.description: Identify and replace the telltale signs of AI-generated interfaces, including placeholder content, generic card layouts, and cliched component patterns, with refined alternatives.

use_cases.title: Audit and fix design debt in a legacy project
use_cases.description: Systematically review an existing project for accessibility gaps, missing interaction states, inconsistent styling, and content problems, then apply prioritized fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Quick design audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill to my project. Read through the codebase and give me a list of all the generic design patterns you find, organized by category. Do not make any changes yet, just show me the audit results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding what needs improvement before committing to changes

prompt_templates.title: Typography and color upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill to my project. Fix all typography issues and color problems you find. Replace generic fonts with better alternatives, fix heading hierarchy, adjust paragraph widths, and clean up the color palette. Work within the existing CSS setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: High-impact visual improvements with low risk

prompt_templates.title: Full redesign pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill to my project. Go through the full audit, then fix issues in priority order: fonts, colors, hover states, layout and spacing, generic components, loading and error states, and typography polish. Keep changes focused and reviewable.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive upgrade of an existing project

prompt_templates.title: Fix AI design fingerprints
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill to my project. Focus specifically on replacing the most common AI-generated design patterns: the purple-blue gradient, three equal card columns, pill badges, accordion FAQs, carousel testimonials, and generic copy. Replace each with a more distinctive alternative.
prompt_templates.scenario: Removing obvious signs that a design was AI-generated

output_examples.input: Running a design audit on a marketing site built with Tailwind CSS
output: Typography: Using Inter for all text, headlines lack weight variation, paragraphs stretch full viewport width
Color: Pure black background with saturated blue accent, mixing warm and cool grays inconsistently
Layout: Three equal card columns for features, everything centered symmetrically, no max-width constraint
States: No hover effects on buttons, no loading or empty states on the contact form
Content: Generic company names, Lorem Ipsum in the blog section, exclamation marks in all success messages

output_examples.input: Upgrading typography on a Next.js dashboard
output: Replaced Inter with Geist for body text and Cabinet Grotesk for headings
Set paragraph max-width to 65ch with increased line-height for readability
Added Medium (500) weight for navigation labels and SemiBold (600) for card titles
Applied font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums to all data table columns
Fixed orphaned words with text-wrap: pretty on all paragraph elements

best_practices: Always audit the existing codebase before making changes to understand the current tech stack, framework version, and styling approach
Apply fixes in priority order for maximum visual impact with minimum risk: fonts first, then colors, then interaction states, then layout
Keep every change small and focused so that pull requests remain easy to review and roll back if needed
anti_patterns: Rewriting the entire project from scratch instead of improving existing code incrementally
Migrating to a new CSS framework or styling library when the current system works fine
Making large sweeping changes in a single commit that are impossible to review or debug

faq.question: Does this skill work with any framework or CSS setup?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill detects your existing framework and styling method, then applies upgrades that work within your current setup. It supports Tailwind CSS, styled-components, vanilla CSS, and other common approaches.

faq.question: Will this break my existing functionality?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to avoid breaking changes. It works with existing code, applies targeted improvements, and follows rules that prioritize compatibility over rewrites.

faq.question: Can I choose which improvements to apply?
faq.answer: Yes. You can request a full audit first to see all identified issues, then ask the skill to fix specific categories like typography, colors, or interaction states.

faq.question: What does this skill look for in a design audit?
faq.answer: It checks typography, color and surfaces, layout, interactivity states, content quality, component patterns, iconography, code quality, and strategic omissions like missing legal links or form validation.

faq.question: Does this create visual mockups or design files?
faq.answer: No. This skill applies design improvements directly to your code as CSS and HTML changes. It does not produce Figma files, images, or visual mockups.

faq.question: How does this differ from a general design assistant?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on upgrading existing projects rather than creating new ones from scratch. It identifies and replaces common AI-generated design fingerprints with more distinctive, premium alternatives.
